original_abstract: Efficient adaptation of Large Language Models (LLMs) to specialized downstream tasks is often hindered by the substantial computational resources required and the technical complexity of implementing diverse fine-tuning methodologies. We introduce LLAMAFACTORY, an open-source, unified framework designed to democratize and streamline the efficient fine-tuning of over 100 language models. The framework integrates a comprehensive suite of cutting-edge optimization techniques—such as LoRA, GaLore, and DoRA—with various training objectives, including supervised fine-tuning (SFT), direct preference optimization (DPO), and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF). A key feature of the framework is LLAMABOARD, a zero-code web interface that allows users to configure experiments, monitor real-time training dynamics, and evaluate model performance without programming. Our empirical evaluations demonstrate that LLAMAFACTORY significantly reduces memory overhead while maintaining high throughput and performance across language modeling and text generation benchmarks. Since its release, the framework has gained significant traction in the research community, serving as a robust foundation for diverse LLM applications and studies.
humanized_abstract: Fine tuning Large Language Models (LLMs) for target tasks such as sentiment analysis and product description generation requires large amounts of computational resources and domain specific knowledge of the fine tuning approaches for achieving desired performance. Thus fine tuning LLMs is not efficient for over 100 models. LLAMAFACTORY is an open source, general-purpose, easy-to-use toolkit that eases the processing for large amounts of LLMs and their efficient fine tuning. We integrate a set of state-of-the-art optimizations (i.e., LoRA, GaLore, and DoRA) along with training modes such as supervised fine-tuning (SFT), direct preference optimization (DPO), and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF). Additionally, we propose a zero-code web interface named as LLAMABOARD. This interface allows users to set up their fine-tuning tasks and model outputs by showing the whole fine-tuning process with real-time updates, eliminating the need to write codes to view the performance of different LLMs and their fine-tuned models. Experiments show that the usage of LLAMAFACTORY leads to the reduction of memory consumption and the maintaining of high-throughput while achieving better results in both language modeling future language and text generation tasks. The community adopted this fantastic platform for many applications and studies of LLMs.
